text:
RADIOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Diane Castellano
Age: 56 years
Study: CT Chest/Abdomen/Pelvis with IV Contrast; MRI Brain with and without Gadolinium Contrast
Indication: Metastatic melanoma, BRAF V600E-mutant. History of hepatic and intracranial metastatic disease. Status post stereotactic radiosurgery to brain metastases and combination immunotherapy (ipilimumab/nivolumab followed by nivolumab maintenance). Restaging at approximately 24 months from initial diagnosis / planned 2-year immunotherapy duration assessment.
Comparison: Prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and MRI brain.

---

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST

TECHNIQUE: Helical CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the administration of 90 mL of Omnipaque 350 intravenous contrast. Images were acquired in the portal venous phase. Multiplanar reformations reviewed.

FINDINGS:

Chest:
Lungs: No pulmonary nodules or masses identified. The airways are patent. No pleural effusion. No pneumothorax.
Mediastinum: Heart size is normal. No pericardial effusion. Mediastinal and hilar contours are unremarkable. No pathologically enlarged mediastinal, hilar, or axillary lymph nodes. Previously noted mediastinal adenopathy has resolved.
Chest wall / soft tissues: Unremarkable.
Upper abdomen (chest windows): Visualized portions of the liver, spleen, and adrenal glands appear unremarkable at the lung base level.

Abdomen:
Liver: The liver is normal in size and demonstrates homogeneous enhancement. Two tiny hypodense foci are identified on portal venous phase imaging in hepatic segments IV and VI, measuring approximately 0.4 cm and 0.5 cm in greatest dimension. These are at the limit of CT resolution and are not confidently characterizable on this study. They are markedly reduced from prior examination when multiple confluent hypodense lesions were present throughout both hepatic lobes. The current foci may represent residual scar / fibrosis, minimally residual viable disease, or small cysts — indeterminate at this size. No new hepatic lesions are identified. No biliary dilatation. Portal vein is patent.
Spleen: Normal in size and attenuation. No focal splenic lesions.
Pancreas: Unremarkable. No ductal dilatation.
Adrenal glands: Bilateral adrenal glands are within normal limits. No adrenal nodules. Previously noted small right adrenal nodule is no longer visualized.
Kidneys: Bilateral kidneys are symmetric and enhance normally. No hydronephrosis. No concerning renal masses.
Stomach / bowel: Loops of small and large bowel are unremarkable. No obstruction. Bowel wall is not thickened. No free intraperitoneal fluid or gas.
Retroperitoneum: No pathologically enlarged retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymph nodes. Previously noted low-volume retroperitoneal adenopathy is no longer evident.
Pelvis: Uterus and adnexa are unremarkable. Urinary bladder is decompressed but the visible wall appears smooth. No pelvic lymphadenopathy. No free fluid.

Musculoskeletal / soft tissue:
No suspicious osseous lesions. No soft tissue masses. Visualized subcutaneous tissues are unremarkable.

IMPRESSION — CT Chest/Abdomen/Pelvis:
1. Dramatic improvement from prior studies. Hepatic disease has undergone marked regression. Two tiny residual hypodense foci in the liver (0.4 and 0.5 cm), at the limit of resolution, are indeterminate — most likely representing residual scar or fibrosis, though minimal residual disease cannot be excluded at this size.
2. No new hepatic lesions.
3. Previously noted retroperitoneal and mediastinal adenopathy has resolved.
4. Right adrenal nodule no longer visualized.
5. No evidence of active extrahepatic thoracic, abdominal, or pelvic disease.

---

MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST

TECHNIQUE: MRI of the brain was performed utilizing standard sequences including sagittal T1, axial FLAIR, axial DWI/ADC, axial T2, axial T1 pre- and post-gadolinium (Gadavist 0.1 mmol/kg IV), and high-resolution 3D MP-RAGE post-contrast. No prior brain MRI available at this institution for direct side-by-side comparison; prior report reviewed.

FINDINGS:

Prior history: Multiple brain metastases treated with stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) at approximately 8 months from diagnosis. Subsequent ipilimumab/nivolumab combination immunotherapy followed by nivolumab maintenance.

There are small areas of T1 hypointensity with associated susceptibility artifact (hemosiderin deposition / hemosiderin staining) in the right parietal and left cerebellar hemisphere, consistent with prior SRS treatment sites. These sites demonstrate no abnormal gadolinium enhancement. No perilesional edema. No mass effect. Appearances are consistent with treated/involuted metastases — no imaging features to suggest viable intracranial tumor at these sites.

No new areas of abnormal parenchymal enhancement are identified. No new parenchymal or leptomeningeal nodules. No sulcal or dural enhancement to suggest leptomeningeal disease.

Ventricular system is normal in size and configuration. No midline shift. No herniation.

Diffusion-weighted imaging: No restricted diffusion to suggest acute ischemia.

T2/FLAIR: Mild scattered periventricular and subcortical white matter T2 hyperintensities, nonspecific, likely reflecting chronic small vessel disease in the appropriate clinical context. No suspicious new FLAIR abnormality.

Calvarium and skull base: No osseous lesions. Paranasal sinuses and mastoid air cells are well aerated.

IMPRESSION — MRI Brain:
1. Bilateral SRS treatment sites (right parietal and left cerebellum) show expected post-treatment involutional changes — hemosiderin deposition, no abnormal enhancement, no edema. No viable intracranial tumor identified.
2. No new brain metastases.
3. No leptomeningeal disease.
4. Mild nonspecific white matter changes, likely chronic small vessel disease.

---

OVERALL IMPRESSION:
Compared with prior imaging spanning the course of treatment, there has been near-complete radiographic response of metastatic melanoma. The only residual finding attributable to known disease is two tiny, sub-centimeter hepatic foci at the limit of resolution, indeterminate but favored to represent scar/fibrosis. No intracranial viable tumor. No lymphadenopathy. No extrahepatic disease.

LDH (per requisition): 148 U/L (within normal limits).

Result reviewed and verified.
Attending Radiologist: [Signature on file]
